name: True Will Concept
description: The True Will, in Thelema, is the central concept representing an individual's unique purpose or path in life. It is considered the 'Great Work' that each person is destined to fulfill. Aleister Crowley, the founder of Thelema, described it as the individual's divine mission, a path of self-discovery and self-actualization aligned with the cosmos. Discovering and following one's True Will is believed to lead to ultimate fulfillment and union with the divine. Proponents argue that identifying the True Will requires introspection, meditation, and often the use of magical practices such as divination and ritual. They believe that societal conditioning and egoic desires often obscure the True Will, leading individuals to pursue paths that are not truly their own. Mainstream perspectives often view the True Will as a psychological construct similar to self-actualization or finding one's passion, but without the spiritual or magical connotations. Critics argue that the subjective nature of the concept makes it difficult to define or validate empirically, and that it can be susceptible to manipulation or misinterpretation. Some critics within occult studies also point out the potential for ego inflation and the blurring of lines between personal desire and divinely ordained purpose within Thelema. The concept draws inspiration from various sources, including Hermeticism, Eastern mysticism, and Western esoteric traditions, aiming to synthesize them into a practical system of self-development.
subtitle: Thelema's core principle of self-discovery

claims.claim: Each individual has a unique True Will.
claims.evidence: Crowley's writings emphasize individual destiny and purpose.
claims.criticism: This claim is not empirically verifiable and relies on subjective interpretation.

claims.claim: Discovering and following the True Will leads to fulfillment and union with the divine.
claims.evidence: Personal accounts and testimonials from Thelemites.
claims.criticism: This claim is based on faith and lacks scientific evidence of divine union.

claims.claim: Magical practices can aid in the discovery of the True Will.
claims.evidence: Use of divination, ritual, and meditation within Thelemic practices.
claims.criticism: The efficacy of magical practices is not scientifically proven and is often attributed to placebo or psychological effects.

keyFigures.name: Aleister Crowley
keyFigures.role: Founder of Thelema and primary exponent of the True Will concept

keyFigures.name: Lon Milo DuQuette
keyFigures.role: Modern Thelemic writer and commentator on Crowley's teachings

keyFigures.name: Richard Kaczynski
keyFigures.role: Biographer of Aleister Crowley, providing historical context

mythologyConnections: Ancient Egyptian religion (references in Liber AL)

timeline.event: Reception of Liber AL vel Legis, foundational text of Thelema, which introduces the concept of True Will
Crowley develops and elaborates on the True Will concept in his writings
The True Will remains a central tenet of Thelema practiced worldwide
